guide_response.verdict: STAGE_3_CLOSE_VERIFIED -- three parallel verification subagents (S1 devplatform technical correctness, S2 BlarAI sole-mutation invariant, S3 procedural completeness) converge on VERIFIED. Five new findings dispositioned via new ack g7-ea7_n14 with sub-clauses (a) through (d). One forward-bound to Stage 4 EA-8 preflight (NEW rr8); three folded into Stage 6.7.5 TICKET E scope expansion; two procedural recommendations bound. None block Stage 4 transition. Stage 3 marked COMPLETE; Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff initiated next turn pending LA authorization.

summary.trigger_context:
EA-7 (Stage 3 Execution Agent, instance 7 per global EA-numbering convention) reported Stage 3 close on 2026-04-28: BlarAI HEAD `76f6050` (single STATUS.md mutation +59 -5; parent `865c52f`); devplatform HEAD `95884a0` (189 files / +39592 -2; parent `3894221`); state.json sha256 `71FC1A2F...D5D19` UNCHANGED; fleet remains paused; stop-conditions (i)(ii)(iii)(iv) NOT triggered, (v) deferred to Stage 4 live-load smoke; acks discharged g7-ea1_n11 + n12 + n13 (sub-clauses a-h); naming defect A12 recorded with verbatim-restatement disposition.
The LA explicitly directed: "mature verification process not minimal one... thrice over with different subagents if that's possible."
summary.verification_methodology:
Three parallel general-purpose subagents spawned by Guide-#7, each verifying one orthogonal slice. Read-only mandate; zero file mutations. Convergence required across all three for VERIFIED disposition. The orthogonal slicing ensures any defect surfaces from at least one angle: - S1 verifies what EA-7 produced INSIDE devplatform (technical correctness; n13 sub-clause discharge; secret-leak check; diff stats) - S2 verifies what EA-7 did NOT touch in BlarAI (sole-mutation invariant; state.json sha256 byte-identity; per-file read-only) - S3 verifies the PROCEDURE (9-step execution_ordering; 11 success_criteria; forward-bind preservation; A12 naming-defect handling)
Full evidence preserved at: `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md`
summary.findings_overview:
All three subagents converge on VERIFIED. Findings: - S1: 5 new findings (A1 state.json contamination, A2 working-tree drift, A3 orphaned VS Code task, A4 manifest totals divergence, A5 stale help text). VERIFIED_WITH_FINDINGS. - S2: 3 findings (F-S2-1 close-report under-enumeration, F-S2-2 pattern-consistent verdict-path omission, F-S2-3 timing-inherent evidence reference). VERIFIED_WITH_FINDINGS. - S3: 2 findings (P1 audit evidence not directly footnoted, P2 rr/f5_ not enumerated verbatim in STATUS). VERIFIED.
NONE of these findings block Stage 4 transition. All are dispositioned below.
summary.ack_naming_note:
The new ack emitted by this verdict is `g7-ea7_n14` per the global EA-numbering convention (Guide-#7 reviewing EA-7's close). This is the FIRST Guide-#7 verdict to use the corrected `ea7` suffix. Prior verdicts (3.0.v2, 3.1.v2, 3.2.v2) used `ea1` per stage-scoped legacy naming; those labels are documentation defects per LA directive 2026-04-27 + EA-7 Anomaly A12 disposition. Per no-amend doctrine, prior verdicts are NOT retroactively rewritten; correction folds into Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A.
Cumulative-ack restatement chain: g7-ea1_n10..n13 (verbatim per no-amend) + g7-ea7_n14 (this verdict, corrected naming).
summary.handoff_trigger:
Stage 3.10 close is the LA-confirmed natural seam for Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff (per chat decision earlier this session). Stage 4 destructive cutover benefits from fresh Guide vigilance; Guide-#7 context budget remains adequate but Stage 4's many sub-items + likely iter cycles risk emergency handoff if deferred.
This verdict marks Stage 3 COMPLETE and authorizes the handoff. The handoff XML is the next deliverable — to be authored on LA confirmation.

triple_subagent_methodology.rationale:
Single-Guide review of a complex multi-amendment stage close has known limitations: (1) confirmation bias — the Guide who authored the binding verdict may overlook execution defects that contradict their assumptions; (2) attention dilution — verifying 8 sub-clauses + 11 success criteria + cross-repo coherence + forward-bind preservation in one pass is error-prone; (3) audit-trail thinness — single review = single point of failure.
Three parallel subagents with orthogonal slices address all three: each subagent has a focused scope (~200-300 lines of mandate), each is uncoupled from the others, and convergence across three independent VERIFIED verdicts is much stronger evidence than a single Guide pass.

subagent_inventory.subagent: Verifies EA-7's PRODUCED artifacts in devplatform: pyproject.toml dep block, .venv installation, MCP config dual-schema preservation + path rewrites + tasks.json broadening, smoke-test execution mode, gitignore augmentation, tools/tests/ inventory + byte-identity, copy_manifest_v2.yaml schema, -Recurse + inverse-verify, diff stats, secret-leak check.

Verifies EA-7's RESPECTED Stage 3 invariants on BlarAI side: only STATUS.md modified (+59 -5), no other BlarAI file touched, state.json sha256 byte-identical pre/post, fleet still paused, scheduled tasks 13/13 disabled, all read-only invariant paths untouched, A12 naming defect handled correctly per no-amend doctrine.

Verifies the META: 9-step execution_ordering followed (timestamps), all 11 Stage 3 success_criteria met, sub-clause discharge claims (n11+n12+n13 a-h) each footnoted in STATUS, forward-bind preservation (rr1-rr7 + f5_1-f5_4 + Tickets A-E all OPEN, none prematurely closed), stop-conditions assessment correct, A12 disposition complete, cross-repo coherence, audit chain integrity.

triple_subagent_methodology.convergence_check:
All three subagents independently produced overlapping evidence on key facts: - devplatform HEAD = 95884a0 (S1 verified; S3 cross-checked via STATUS) - BlarAI HEAD = 76f6050 (S2 verified; S3 cross-checked) - state.json sha256 = 71fc1a2f...d5d19 (S2 verified; S1 confirmed via diff scope) - tools/tests/ has 7 files (S1 enumerated; S3 spot-checked) - VIKUNJA_PASS env-var substitution real (S1 grep; S2 read-only invariant on BlarAI source confirms plaintext source) - A12 naming defect verbatim-restatement (S2 deep-dive; S3 cross-confirmed)
Zero contradictions between subagents. Zero blocking findings. All non-blocking findings cumulatively logged + dispositioned.

subagent_verdicts.subagent_s1_verdict:
VERDICT: VERIFIED_WITH_FINDINGS
All 8 binding sub-clauses (n13(a)-(h)) technically discharged on devplatform side with verifiable evidence. Per-sub-clause: a/b/c/d/f/h = PASS unconditionally. e = PARTIAL (gitignore augmentation present in commit; working-tree-clean false post-commit; state.json contamination unflagged). g = PASS_WITH_FINDING (manifest schema correct; totals/git-count divergence noted).
Five new findings: - A1: tools/autonomy_budget/state.json committed to devplatform with BlarAI's runtime pause-state snapshot - A2: working tree NOT clean post-Stage-3-close — `.gitignore` has 7-line duplicated tail block (functionally inert; EA-7's "Working tree: clean" claim is FALSE at audit time) - A3: `.vscode/tasks.json` references `tools/openvino_contrib_agent/main.py` which does NOT exist in devplatform (orphan task — byte-identical-copy side effect from n13(c)(iii)) - A4: copy_manifest_v2.yaml totals (cumulative_files: 204, v2_files: 195) diverge from git-level enumeration (189 / 196) by ~15 - A5: daily_digest --help still says "defaults to BlarAI" in argparse help text (stale post-extraction)
Secret leak check: ZERO leaks. BlarAI source confirmed-plaintext-original; devplatform copies are env-var. Redaction transform is genuine, not vacuous.
subagent_verdicts.subagent_s2_verdict:
VERDICT: VERIFIED_WITH_FINDINGS
BlarAI-side sole-mutation invariant HONORED in strict technical sense: - Exactly 1 commit between parent 865c52f and HEAD 76f6050 - Exactly 1 file changed (docs/platform_separation/STATUS.md) - +59 -5 lines, exactly matching EA-7's close report - state.json sha256 71fc1a2f21177f2461a750d0239244e709cf1e228778c46a6e188cc50d2d5d19 UNCHANGED - 13/13 scheduled tasks Disabled - All read-only-invariant paths (pyproject.toml, .mcp.json, .vscode/, tools/, docs/governance/, docs/FLEET_DASHBOARD.md, docs/runbooks/, tools/tests/) UNTOUCHED - Working tree clean - A12 naming-defect verbatim-restatement disposition correctly applied (NO silent correction; consistent verbatim usage of g7-ea1_n* throughout discharge text; forward-binding to 6.7.5 TICKET A explicit)
Three findings: - F-S2-1: EA-7's close report under-enumerated anomaly set (mentioned only A12, but committed A12+A13+A14). A13 (F3 runbook relocation) and A14 (governance subset acknowledgment) are well-formed and appropriate; the close-report messaging gap is communication-level only. - F-S2-2: STATUS.md prose does not quote explicit verdict-file path (pattern-consistent with Stage 2 closure footnotes) - F-S2-3: Audit subagent evidence reference timing-inherent absent (cannot reference future evidence)
subagent_verdicts.subagent_s3_verdict:
VERDICT: VERIFIED (with minor cosmetic findings only)
EA-7's Stage 3 close is procedurally complete and forward-bind preservation is intact: - 9-step execution_ordering: All steps executed in prescribed order (timestamp evidence) - 11/11 success_criteria: All verified against disk evidence and STATUS.md - All 11 acks discharged with footnote evidence (n11 in §2, n12 in §3, n13 sub-clauses (a)-(h) in §4) - All 16 forward-bind items preserved: 7 rr + 4 f5_ + 5 Tickets — none prematurely closed - Stop-conditions assessment correct: (i)-(iv) NOT triggered; (v) deferral procedurally sound - A12 naming defect handled per no-amend doctrine (verbatim restatement; TICKET A scope expansion) - Cross-repo coherence verified (BlarAI ↔ devplatform commit cross-references match) - Audit chain integrity verified (all 4 canonical verdict files git-tracked)
Two cosmetic findings (non-blocking): - P1: Audit evidence MD file referenced in verdict line 668 but not directly enumerated in STATUS Footnote §4. Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A could absorb a footnote-cross-reference enrichment. - P2: STATUS.md does NOT enumerate rr1-rr7 / f5_1-f5_4 verbatim. Reachable only via verdict file. Recommendation: Stage 4 EA initialization XML must explicitly forward the list.
subagent_verdicts.triple_convergence_verdict:
All three subagents converge on VERIFIED. The "WITH_FINDINGS" qualifier on S1 and S2 reflects 5+3=8 minor disk-state observations; S3 found 2 additional cosmetic procedural items. Dispositions in §findings_disposition below.
Triple convergence increases confidence beyond single-Guide review: each subagent independently confirmed the core facts (commit hashes, sha256, file counts, sub-clause discharge) AND each surfaced findings the others did not (S1 found A1-A5 disk-state; S2 found close-report under-enumeration; S3 found cross-repo + procedural items).
Stage 3 close is VERIFIED. Stage 4 EA-8 spawn is procedurally authorized.

findings_disposition.preamble: All findings are dispositioned non-optionally per LA mature-not-minimal motto + feedback memory ("Hardening followups are NON-OPTIONAL — both documentation AND architectural cleanup"). LA may triage priority at execution time; existence of disposition is required.

finding.description: tools/autonomy_budget/state.json committed to devplatform carrying BlarAI's runtime pause-state snapshot (fleet_paused=true, last_updated_utc=2026-04-24T20:46:12Z, last_updated_by=copilot_agent).
finding.disposition: Already covered by existing forward-carry rr3 in the 3.0.v2 verdict (§forward_carry_to_stage_4_ea: "State.json contamination at devplatform"). Stage 4 EA-8 MUST decide explicitly: (a) reset to a clean devplatform baseline at first wake (lifts cleared, pause cleared, telemetry zeroed), or (b) carry the BlarAI snapshot forward and let next state-update natural-resolve. Strong recommendation: explicit reset, with provenance note in Stage 4 STATUS append.

finding.description: devplatform working tree NOT clean post-Stage-3-close: `.gitignore` has 7-line tail block duplicated. EA-7's "Working tree: clean" claim is FALSE at audit time. Functionally inert (gitignore tolerates duplicates) but real working-tree drift.
finding.disposition: NEW forward-carry rr8 added to Stage 4 EA-8 residual-risk list. Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL run `git checkout -- .gitignore` (or equivalent) on devplatform AS THE FIRST PREFLIGHT ACTION before any Stage 4 work. Verify working tree clean post-cleanup. If duplication has further propagated (e.g., subsequent Claude agent re-ran the augmentation again), normalize the file content + commit as Stage 4 housekeeping commit.

finding.description: devplatform/.vscode/tasks.json references `tools/openvino_contrib_agent/main.py` which does NOT exist in devplatform (BlarAI-only directory; not in n13(c)(iii) byte-identical-copy expectation but copied as side effect). Result: orphaned task entry in VS Code task runner.
finding.disposition: Stage 6.7.5 TICKET E scope expanded to include this cleanup. EA at Stage 6.7.5 SHALL either (a) port `tools/openvino_contrib_agent/` for devplatform if scope-warranted, OR (b) delete the orphaned task entry from devplatform/.vscode/tasks.json. LA may triage priority at Stage 6 execution time.

finding.description: devplatform/.copy_manifest_v2.yaml `totals.cumulative_files_at_stage_3_close: 204` and `totals.v2_files: 195` diverge from git-level enumeration (`git ls-files | wc -l` = 196; `git diff-tree` for commit 95884a0 = 189). Difference ~15 files. The manifest is a directory-aggregated provenance view, not a 1:1 file enumeration; n13(g) text accepted "(~20 explicit entries)" approximation.
finding.disposition: Stage 6.7.5 TICKET E scope expanded: EA at Stage 6.7.5 SHALL add a `notes:` block clarification to `.copy_manifest_v2.yaml` documenting the directory-rollup-vs-git-file-count semantic (so future auditors don't expect 1:1 alignment), OR optionally regenerate the manifest with explicit per-file enumeration if posterity-rigor preferred.
finding.new_binding: g7-ea7_n14(b) — see new_acknowledgment below (folded with A3 + A5).

finding.description: daily_digest --help still prints "Project root directory (defaults to BlarAI)". Hard-coded BlarAI default in argparse help text is stale post-extraction. Not a Stage 3 scope item per n13(d) wording (which only required --help to exit 0 cleanly).
finding.disposition: Stage 6.7.5 TICKET E scope expanded: EA at Stage 6.7.5 SHALL rewrite the argparse help text to be project-agnostic (e.g., "Project root directory (defaults to auto-detect from cwd or PROJECT_ROOT env var)") + verify the --help output reflects the change. May fold into broader posterity-cleanup pass that scans all platform-tool argparse help-text strings for stale BlarAI references.
finding.new_binding: g7-ea7_n14(b) — see new_acknowledgment below (folded with A3 + A4).

finding.description: EA-7's close report enumerated only A12 (naming defect) but the Stage 3 STATUS append committed three new anomalies: A12 + A13 (F3_BASH_FORK_ERROR_RUNBOOK relocation BlarAI docs/ → devplatform docs/runbooks/) + A14 (selective governance subset 4-of-17 + Stage 3.3 expansion acknowledgment). A13 and A14 are well-formed, appropriate, follow the four-section anomaly structure used by A1-A11.
finding.disposition: No ticket required. The anomalies themselves are appropriate; only the close-report messaging was incomplete. Note for posterity: Stage 4+ EA close reports should enumerate ALL new anomalies recorded in the Stage commit, not just the ones flagged in the Guide verdict's expected list. This is a communication-doctrine clarification, not a defect requiring ticket creation.
finding.new_binding: None. Procedural observation only; bound informally via this verdict's record.

finding.description: STATUS.md Stage 3 Closure Footnotes prose says "the substantive record in the Guide-#7 verdict files and the Stage 3 EA acknowledgment chain" without quoting explicit file path. Pattern-consistent with Stage 2 Closure Footnotes (which similarly does not cite specific verdict-file path in §-header).
finding.disposition: No action required. Pattern-consistent omission. STATUS.md is intentionally a high-density living state file; the verdict files are authoritative records cited by ack id (cumulative-ack chain provides reachability). LA may optionally request inline path citation in future closure footnotes; until then, the existing pattern is acceptable.

finding.description: STATUS append cannot reference the audit subagent evidence file (3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md) because that file did not exist at the time of EA-7's commit (76f6050). Timing-inherent.
finding.disposition: No defect. The verdict's evidence reference is forward-only (committed by Guide-#7 alongside this verdict, AFTER EA-7's close commit). Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer will reference the evidence + verdict together.

finding.description: Audit evidence MD file (3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_DRIFT_INVENTORY.md) referenced in 3.0.v2 verdict line 668 but not directly enumerated in STATUS.md Footnote §4. Reachable via verdict cross-reference; not directly footnoted.
finding.disposition: Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A scope already includes posterity-correction of locked verdict artifacts; expand to also add cross-reference pointers in STATUS.md from each Closure Footnotes section to the relevant audit-evidence MD file paths. This applies to: - Stage 3 Closure Footnotes → 3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT (drift inventory) + 3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3 (close verification) - Future stages: same pattern.

finding.description: STATUS.md Stage 3 Closure Footnotes does NOT enumerate rr1-rr7 (Stage 4 forward-carry residual risk items) or f5_1-f5_4 (Stage 5 forward-carry) verbatim. Reachable only via verdict file (3.0.v2). Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must explicitly forward these for redundancy.
finding.disposition: REQUIREMENT bound for Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff XML AND for the Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer (which Guide-#8 will author): both artifacts MUST enumerate rr1-rr8 (now including new rr8 from A2) and f5_1-f5_4 verbatim, not just by reference. This ensures Stage 4 EA-8 reads the residual-risk list directly without needing to cross-walk back to the 3.0.v2 verdict.

new_acknowledgment.title: Stage 3.10 closure verification — new findings disposition + Stage 4 forward-carry expansion
new_acknowledgment.preamble:
g7-ea7_n14 is the FIRST verdict ack to use the corrected global EA-numbering convention (`ea7` suffix) per LA directive 2026-04-27. Prior verdicts (3.0.v2, 3.1.v2, 3.2.v2) used `ea1` per stage-scoped legacy naming; those labels are documentation defects per Anomaly A12 disposition + LA directive. Per no-amend doctrine, prior verdicts are NOT retroactively rewritten; correction folds into Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A.
The four sub-clauses (a-d) bind dispositions for findings surfaced by the three-subagent verification:
- n14(a) Stage 4 EA-8 NEW forward-carry rr8 (.gitignore working-tree cleanup preflight) - n14(b) Stage 6.7.5 TICKET E scope expansion (3 new sub-items: A3 orphaned VS Code task, A4 manifest totals notes, A5 daily_digest help text) - n14(c) Stage 4 init pointer requirement (rr1-rr8 + f5_1-f5_4 verbatim enumeration) - n14(d) Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A enrichment (audit-evidence cross-references in STATUS Closure Footnotes)
All four sub-clauses are REQUIRED. No sub-clause is optional. (Per LA mature-not-minimal motto + feedback memory: hardening followups non-optional for both documentation AND architectural cleanup.)

subclause.title: Stage 4 EA-8 NEW forward-carry rr8 — gitignore working-tree cleanup preflight

new_acknowledgment.subclause:
Finding A2: devplatform working tree NOT clean post-Stage-3-close. `.gitignore` has 7-line tail block duplicated. EA-7's "Working tree: clean" claim is FALSE at audit time. Functionally inert (gitignore tolerates duplicates) but real working-tree drift.
AMENDMENT: NEW residual-risk item rr8 added to Stage 4 EA-8 forward-carry list. Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL execute the following AS THE FIRST PREFLIGHT ACTION before any Stage 4 work begins:
```powershell cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform git status --porcelain # Expected output: ` M .gitignore` (or empty if some other agent cleaned up) git diff .gitignore # Expected output: 7-line tail block duplicated (see audit evidence S1 §A2)
# Decision A (recommended): revert to committed state git checkout -- .gitignore git status --porcelain # Expected: empty (clean working tree)
# Decision B (if duplication has further propagated): # Manually edit .gitignore to dedupe; commit as Stage 4 housekeeping. ```
EXIT CRITERION: working tree clean on devplatform before proceeding with Stage 4 cutover preflight.
RATIONALE: Stage 4 EA-8 will make many changes to devplatform (cutover MCP repointing, scheduled task re-enablement). Inheriting an uncommitted working-tree state risks: (a) mistakenly committing the duplication when the EA does its first `git add .`; (b) confusing Stage 4 EA-8 about whether the duplication was an intentional mid-stage state. Clean-baseline-first is procedurally cleaner.
FORWARD-CARRY: rr8 added to the Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer's forward-carry-from-Stage-3 section (per n14(c) verbatim-enumeration requirement). Guide-#8 successor will inherit and forward.

subclause.title: Stage 6.7.5 TICKET E scope expansion — 3 new sub-items

new_acknowledgment.subclause:
Findings A3, A4, A5 are minor cosmetic-or-cleanup items appropriate for Stage 6.7.5 TICKET E (originally scoped at g7-ea1_n13 to absorb the 3.0.v2 audit-checkpoint amendments into posterity XML cleanup).
AMENDMENT: TICKET E scope expanded to include 3 new sub-items:
**TICKET E sub-item (i) — orphaned VS Code task in devplatform/.vscode/tasks.json** Source: S1 finding A3. EA at Stage 6.7.5 SHALL either: - (a) Port `tools/openvino_contrib_agent/` to devplatform if scope-warranted (cross-reference: BlarAI's openvino_contrib_agent is BlarAI-only per Stage 5.1 keep_in_blarai list; "port" likely means "remove from devplatform tasks.json"), OR - (b) Delete the orphaned task entry from devplatform/.vscode/tasks.json (recommended per A3 finding's classification as orphan). Verify via `Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\tools\openvino_contrib_agent` after action; expect False (path doesn't exist; orphan task is the only correct remaining state).
**TICKET E sub-item (ii) — copy_manifest_v2.yaml totals divergence notes** Source: S1 finding A4. EA at Stage 6.7.5 SHALL add a `notes:` block clarification to `devplatform/.copy_manifest_v2.yaml` documenting the directory-rollup-vs-git-file-count semantic. Recommended note text:
```yaml notes: | Generated for Stage 5 cleanup audit per Stage 3 item 3.10.v2. Totals reflect manifest-curated directory-rollup view; counts do NOT 1:1 align with git-level file enumeration (git ls-files vs git diff-tree). Manifest is a provenance view of v1+v2 source contributions, not a tracked-files inventory. Reconciliation example at Stage 3 close: cumulative_files: 204 (manifest rollup) vs 196 (git ls-files) vs 189 (git diff-tree for the Stage 3 commit). ```
Optionally regenerate manifest with explicit per-file enumeration if posterity-rigor preferred (likely NOT required; the rollup is intentional).
**TICKET E sub-item (iii) — daily_digest --help text BlarAI staleness** Source: S1 finding A5. EA at Stage 6.7.5 SHALL rewrite argparse help text in `tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py` to be project-agnostic. Recommended replacement:
```python parser.add_argument("--project-root", default=None, help="Project root directory (defaults to auto-detect from cwd via _project_context.resolve())") ```
May fold into broader posterity-cleanup pass that scans all platform-tool argparse help-text strings for stale BlarAI references (likely 1-3 additional similar strings across the 13 entrypoints; full scan candidate).
LA may triage priority at Stage 6 execution time. Sub-items (i), (ii), (iii) all REQUIRED per LA mature-not-minimal motto; ticket EXISTENCE is non-optional.

subclause.title: Stage 4 init pointer requirement — rr1-rr8 + f5_1-f5_4 verbatim enumeration

new_acknowledgment.subclause:
Finding P2: STATUS.md does NOT enumerate rr1-rr7 / f5_1-f5_4 verbatim. Reachable only via 3.0.v2 verdict file. Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer must explicitly forward these for redundancy.
AMENDMENT: REQUIREMENT bound for both: - Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff XML (next deliverable, pending LA authorization) - Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer (authored by Guide-#8 successor)
Both artifacts MUST enumerate rr1-rr8 verbatim (now including new rr8 from n14(a)) AND f5_1-f5_4 verbatim, not just by reference. The Stage 4 EA-8 must be able to read its forward-carry list WITHOUT needing to cross-walk back to the 3.0.v2 verdict file.
The handoff XML and init pointer both reference the cumulative-ack chain g7-ea1_n10..n13 (verbatim per no-amend) + g7-ea7_n14 (this verdict) but the rr/f5_ items are spec-content that benefits from direct enumeration in the active artifact.
EXIT CRITERION: handoff XML and init pointer both contain enumerated rr1-rr8 + f5_1-f5_4 sections (each item: id, priority, description, target_stage, status_at_handoff).

subclause.title: Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A enrichment — audit evidence cross-references in STATUS Closure Footnotes

new_acknowledgment.subclause:
Finding P1: Audit evidence MD files (3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT and now 3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3) are referenced in verdicts but not directly enumerated in STATUS.md Closure Footnotes sections.
AMENDMENT: Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A scope expanded to absorb cross-reference enrichment.
Original TICKET A scope (per g7-ea1_n12 + g7-ea1_n13(c)(i) + EA-7 A12 disposition): consolidated locked-artifact amendments — fold (a) verdict-text typos and spec drifts, (b) EA-numbering rewrite (g7-ea1_n* → g7-ea7_n*) across the 4 committed verdict files, (c) Stage 3.X audit-checkpoint amendments fold into posterity XML cleanup.
Expanded scope per n14(d): also add cross-reference pointers in STATUS.md from each Closure Footnotes section to the relevant audit-evidence MD file paths. Specifically:
- **Stage 3 Closure Footnotes** → enrich §1 (Naming defect) with explicit pointer to `temp_for_responses/3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_DRIFT_INVENTORY.md` AND `temp_for_responses/3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md` - **Future Stage Closure Footnotes** (Stage 4, 5, 6 close) → same pattern; each Closure Footnotes section should cross-reference any audit-evidence MD files committed alongside the closure verdict.
LA may triage priority. Existence non-optional.

stage_3_complete.stage_ledger_row: Stage 3 row in STATUS.md Stage Ledger (line 51) is correctly marked COMPLETE per S2 + S3 verifications. Row content: - Status: COMPLETE - Date: 2026-04-28 (UTC) - BlarAI commit: 76f6050 (this STATUS append commit; recover via `git log --oneline -1`) - devplatform commit: 95884a0 (single Stage-3 commit; 189 files / +39592 -2 / parent 3894221) - Key evidence: pointer to Stage 3 Closure Footnotes
stage_3_complete.state_summary: - BlarAI HEAD: `76f6050` on `chore/platform-extraction` - devplatform HEAD: `95884a0` on `main` - state.json sha256: `71fc1a2f21177f2461a750d0239244e709cf1e228778c46a6e188cc50d2d5d19` UNCHANGED across the entire 4-day Stage 2 → Stage 3 procedure cycle - Fleet: paused (`fleet_paused=true`, `last_updated_utc=2026-04-24T20:46:12Z`) - Scheduled tasks: 13/13 Disabled - Vikunja: still running per g6-ea6_n8 Option B (stop scheduled at Stage 4.0/4.1 preflight per rr6) - Working tree (BlarAI): clean - Working tree (devplatform): NOT clean (`M .gitignore` per A2; cleanup bound at n14(a) rr8 Stage 4 preflight)
stage_3_complete.stage_4_transition_authorization:
Stage 4 EA-8 spawn is procedurally AUTHORIZED following Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff. Stage 4 is destructive cutover (Vikunja DB migration, MCP repointing, scheduled-task re-enable, S4U elevation handling for Agents Cadence Monitor); fresh Guide-#8 vigilance is the architectural defense against Stage 4 risks per LA-confirmed handoff timing earlier this session.
Stage 4 EA-8 must NOT spawn until: (1) this verdict committed, (2) Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff XML authored + LA-approved + committed, (3) Guide-#8 instance bootstrapped via the handoff XML, (4) Guide-#8 authors fresh Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer.

forward_carry_to_stage_4_ea.preamble: Stage 4 EA-8 inherits the following residual-risk items. Per n14(c), all items below MUST be enumerated verbatim in the Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff XML AND in the Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer (NOT just referenced by id).
forward_carry_to_stage_4_ea.residual_risk_item: Runtime behavior of platform tools post-MCP-repointing. Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL run an end-to-end MCP smoke test against real Vikunja from devplatform context post-repointing (e.g., `mcp__vikunja__list_projects` round-trip). Surfaces stop-condition (v) deferred from Stage 3 (n13(d)).

Cross-file consistency of path rewrites. Stage 4 EA-8 preflight SHALL grep `C:\\Users\\mrbla\\BlarAI\\` across `devplatform/.mcp.json` and `devplatform/.vscode/mcp.json` and assert zero matches. Any remaining BlarAI path is a Stage 3.6 missed rewrite. (Note: S1 verification at Stage 3 close found ZERO matches in target configs — rr2 is a defense-in-depth re-check at Stage 4 timing.)

State.json contamination at devplatform. EA-7 left BlarAI's runtime pause-state snapshot in devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json (per S1 finding A1; verified). Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL decide explicitly: (a) reset to clean devplatform baseline (recommended), or (b) carry-forward and natural-resolve. Document decision in Stage 4 STATUS append.

__pycache__ pollution. 12 __pycache__/ dirs in `devplatform/tools/` are gitignored but linger on disk. Stage 5 EA SHALL run `Remove-Item -Recurse devplatform/tools/**/__pycache__` before final verification. (Stage 5, not Stage 4 — keeping for completeness.)

Anomaly A3 — `Agents Cadence Monitor` S4U elevation requirement. Stage 4.7 `Unregister-ScheduledTask` + `Register-ScheduledTask` for this task requires elevated PowerShell (LogonType=S4U vs Interactive for the other 12). Stage 4 spec must guard. CRITICAL for Stage 4.7 per Stage 0 anomaly recording.

Vikunja shutdown gate at Stage 4.0/4.1 (g6-ea6_n8 Option B forward direction). Fleet-paused state must be re-verified, AND Vikunja process must be stopped (V4=1 → V4=0 transition). Stage 4 spec already has fail-fast guard at Item 4.1 (`if ($running) { throw }`).
Worktrees-empty precondition (INFRA_DELTA_v2 §3.2). `git worktree list` on BlarAI must show only main worktree before any destructive Stage 4 operation.

devplatform working-tree drift. EA-7's "Working tree: clean" claim was FALSE at Stage 3 close audit time (per S1 finding A2): `M .gitignore` with 7-line duplicated tail block. Functionally inert but real drift. Stage 4 EA-8 SHALL execute `git checkout -- .gitignore` (or equivalent dedup-then-commit) AS THE FIRST PREFLIGHT ACTION before any Stage 4 work.
forward_carry_to_stage_4_ea.escalating_discovery_warning: Per audit subagent §6 residual_risk #6 (audit_evidence MD), the escalating-discovery pattern (each iter finds a new class) suggests Stage 4 EA-8's primary audit may surface 2-3 additional drift classes not anticipated by either the 3.0.v2 audit checkpoint or this 3.10 closure verification. Stage 4 EA-8 shall be empowered to STOP-and-disposition new classes as discovered. The cumulative-ack inheritance vehicle remains available; Stage 4 EA-8 may bind new acks under the `g8-ea8_n*` namespace (per global EA-numbering convention; LA directive 2026-04-27).

forward_carry_to_stage_5_ea.preamble: Stage 5 EA inherits the following forward-items. Per n14(c), enumerated verbatim in handoff XML + Stage 4 init pointer + (eventually) Stage 5 EA init pointer.
forward_carry_to_stage_5_ea.forward_item: F-N5/F-N6/F-N7 path drifts (orphan fleet files, ea_queue, top-level no-ops) deferred to Stage 5 EA fresh-eyes. No changes from this verdict.

F-N8 (BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json post-Stage-5 cleanup) — n13(c)(iii) closed the dependency: devplatform now has its own .vscode/tasks.json with the Vikunja Bridge entry, so Stage 5 may delete BlarAI/.vscode/tasks.json's bridge entry without breaking devplatform's bridge launch.

F-N10 (Stage 5.6 testpaths-removal pattern miss for `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests`). Stage 5 EA's testpaths-removal pattern at Stage 5.6 MUST include `tools/gate_stale_cleaner/tests` alongside the originally-listed `tools/vikunja_mcp`, `tools/fleet_*`, `tools/autonomy_budget`. Without this, post-Stage-5 BlarAI pytest collection will fail on missing testpath.

Stage 5.1 chokepoint deletion (BlarAI/tools/_vikunja_client.py + BlarAI/tools/_project_context.py) requires concomitant deletion of BlarAI/tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py + test_project_context.py (their imports break post-deletion). Stage 5 EA shall verify devplatform's copies still run their respective test suites against devplatform's chokepoint copies, then proceed with BlarAI tools/tests/ removal.

stage_6_7_5_hardening_tickets_updated.preamble: Per LA mature-not-minimal motto + feedback memory (hardening followups NON-OPTIONAL for both documentation AND architectural cleanup), this verdict reinforces the 5 existing tickets and expands TICKET A + TICKET E scope. All tickets REQUIRED; LA may triage priority at Stage 6 execution.
stage_6_7_5_hardening_tickets_updated.ticket: Consolidated locked-artifact amendments. Original scope from g7-ea1_n12 covered Stage 3 XML, INFRA_DELTA_v2.md, etc. typo + spec drift correction. Plus per A12 disposition: EA-numbering rewrite (`g7-ea1_n*` → `g7-ea7_n*`) across the 4 committed verdict files (3.1.v2, 3.2.v2, 3.0.v2, 3.10) for posterity. Plus per n14(d): cross-reference pointers in STATUS.md Closure Footnotes to audit-evidence MD files (3.0.v2-AUDIT_SUBAGENT_OUTPUT, 3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3, future Stage 4/5/6 evidence files).

Stage 3.X audit-checkpoint amendments fold into posterity XML cleanup batch (original scope from g7-ea1_n13: fold n13(a)-(h) amendments into locked Stage 3 XML).
Expanded per n14(b) — 3 new sub-items: - **Sub-item (i)**: orphaned VS Code task in devplatform/.vscode/tasks.json (delete tools/openvino_contrib_agent task entry; cross-reference Stage 5.1 keep_in_blarai) - **Sub-item (ii)**: copy_manifest_v2.yaml totals divergence — add `notes:` block clarification documenting directory-rollup-vs-git-file-count semantic - **Sub-item (iii)**: daily_digest --help text BlarAI staleness — rewrite argparse help to project-agnostic; optional broader scan of platform-tool argparse strings

required_acknowledgments.preamble: Cumulative-ack chain at Stage 3 close. Acks must be restated verbatim by Guide-#8 successor + Stage 4 EA-8 + all subsequent agents. Note dual-naming: g7-ea1_n* labels (verbatim per no-amend for committed verdicts 3.0.v2, 3.1.v2, 3.2.v2) and g7-ea7_n14 (corrected naming for this verdict).
required_acknowledgments.ack: OQ-1 V0i HEAD drift dispositioned as STATUS-staleness; Iter 1 EA-7 gate APPROVED.
Item 3.1.v2 escalation_watchdog.ps1 spec-correction (option a: hyphen → underscore).

Item 3.2.v2 selective governance copy (3 fleet-only files copied; 14 BlarAI-runtime files preserved) + 11 noted-for-Stage-EA findings (F-N1..F-N11) + two-layer audit binding (Stage 4 PRIMARY + Stage 5 SECONDARY) + 4 Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets (A-D).

Stage 3.X comprehensive audit checkpoint amendments — Items 3.4 through 3.10.v2 spec-correction binding, sub-clauses (a) through (h). Discharged at EA-7 Stage 3 close (76f6050) per Stage 3 Closure Footnotes §4.

Stage 3.10 closure verification — three-subagent convergence on VERIFIED. Five new findings dispositioned via sub-clauses (a-d): (a) NEW rr8 Stage 4 preflight gitignore cleanup; (b) Stage 6.7.5 TICKET E scope expansion (3 new sub-items); (c) Stage 4 init pointer requirement (rr1-rr8 + f5_1-f5_4 verbatim enumeration); (d) Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A enrichment (audit-evidence cross-references). FIRST verdict to use corrected `ea7` global naming convention per LA directive 2026-04-27.

handoff_recommendation.verdict: RECOMMEND Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff at next turn pending LA authorization.
handoff_recommendation.rationale:
Stage 3.10 close is the LA-confirmed natural seam (per chat decision earlier this session). Handoff now hits all four criteria from the prior recommendation:
1. **Matches Stage 2→3 precedent** (Guide-#6 → Guide-#7 at Stage 2 close, commit `0d61581`). 2. **Clean audit-chain breakpoint**. Stage 3 marked COMPLETE; cumulative ack chain pinned (g7-ea1_n10..n13 + g7-ea7_n14); no mid-disposition state. 3. **Stage 4 destructive cutover benefits from fresh Guide-#8 vigilance**. Vikunja DB migrate + MCP repoint + S4U elevation + scheduled-task re-enable. 4. **Context budget headroom**. Guide-#7 has authored 3 substantive verdicts (3.0.v2 + 3.10 + parallel handoff XML draft) plus 4 subagent dispatches in this session resumed. Stage 4's many sub-items + likely iter cycles risk emergency handoff if deferred.
handoff_recommendation.handoff_xml_requirements:
The Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff XML (next deliverable) MUST encode:
- **Cumulative ack chain** g7-ea1_n10..n13 verbatim (per no-amend for committed legacy naming) + g7-ea7_n14 (this verdict, corrected naming). With explicit naming-convention note: Guide-#8 should emit new acks under `g8-ea8_n*` per global convention. - **Stage 3.X audit-checkpoint methodology** as procedural inheritance — Guide-#8 should expect Stage 4 to need a similar pre-flight audit before EA-8 begins, since the locked Stage 4 XML is even older relative to disk evolution. - **Forward-carry to Stage 4 EA-8** (rr1-rr8 verbatim enumerated) and **Stage 5 forward-items** (f5_1-f5_4 verbatim enumerated) per n14(c) requirement. - **5 Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets** (A through E with current scope including EA-numbering rewrite + audit-evidence cross-refs + 3 new TICKET E sub-items). - **EA-numbering convention reaffirmation** so Guide-#8 doesn't propagate the ea1 defect. - **BlarAI HEAD + devplatform HEAD** at Stage 3 close + state.json contents (fleet still paused, by whom, sha256). - **Triple-subagent verification methodology** as procedural inheritance — Guide-#8 should consider triple-verification at high-risk closure points (e.g., Stage 4 close pre-Stage-5 cleanup).

next_action_for_la.step_1: Review this closure verdict + the audit evidence: - `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\platform_separation\temp_for_responses\3.10-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml` (this verdict) - `C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\platform_separation\temp_for_responses\3.10-AUDIT_SUBAGENTS_S1_S2_S3_STAGE3_CLOSE_VERIFICATION_EVIDENCE.md` (three-subagent evidence)
next_action_for_la.step_2: Authorize one of the following paths: - **(Recommended) Guide-#7 to Guide-#8 handoff** — say "approve handoff" or similar. Guide-#7 will author the handoff XML next turn; LA will receive a paste-ready Guide-#8 init prompt + the handoff XML; LA spawns fresh Guide-#8 instance. - **Stage 4 EA-8 spawn under Guide-#7 continued tenure** — declines handoff. Guide-#7 stays on through Stage 4. Risk: context budget tightening; emergency handoff mid-Stage-4 possible. - **Further analysis or scope adjustment** — LA-directed, e.g., a fourth subagent verifying a specific concern, or rejecting one of the dispositions, or expanding TICKET E further.
next_action_for_la.step_3:
Once Guide-#8 spawned (per recommended path), Guide-#8 will author Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer (per n14(c) verbatim-enumeration requirement). Stage 4 EA-8 then runs comprehension gate against the init pointer + handoff XML + this 3.10 verdict.
Stage 4 destructive cutover work begins under Guide-#8's tenure.
next_action_for_la.pause_state_note: Fleet remains paused (`fleet_paused=true`, `last_updated_utc=2026-04-24T20:46:12Z`, `last_updated_by=copilot_agent`) throughout the handoff and Stage 4. Per memory feedback: fleet pause is LA-coordinated across sessions; Guide-#7 will not auto-unpause; Guide-#8 successor will inherit the pause state and is bound by the same memory rule.
